channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: One dead, nine wounded in Russian strikes on Ukraine's Kherson
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Russian glide bombs and artillery struck a city in southern Ukraine on Wednesday, killing one person and wounding nine others as Moscow forces continued daily attacks across the country.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: White House removes wire spot from press pool
item.description: The White House is making changes to which outlets are included in the press pool covering President Trump, and doing away with the spot normally reserved for wire services covering his daily activities.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Starbucks introducing new dress code for baristas, a move protested by some
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code this week.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KREX 5

item.title: AP Was There: A truck bomb rips through a federal building in Oklahoma City in 1995
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — EDITORS NOTE: On April 19, 1995, a former U.S. Army soldier parked a rented Ryder truck loaded with a powerful bomb made of fertilizer and fuel oil outside a federal office building in Oklahoma City. The blast at the Alfred P.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New Jersey Transit train engineers reject labor deal with management
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (AP) — New Jersey Transit train engineers have rejected a labor agreement with management, raising the potential for a strike or a lockout next month.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Man in tactical gear attacks officers with pepper spray at Boston-area courthouse, video shows
item.description: Police in a Boston suburb have arrested a man who they say entered a courthouse while wearing a gas mask and tactical gear and attacked several people with pepper spray.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: An Ethiopian Orthodox Church in Washington preserves ancient traditions amid modern challenges
item.description: In the U.S. capital, hundreds of Ethiopians dressed in white tunics gather at the DSK Mariam Church to chant and pray in an ancient liturgical language of one of the oldest branches of Christianity.
item.dc:creator: Luis Andres Henao, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Jury is picked for Karen Read's retrial over death of police boyfriend, opening statements next week
item.description: The jury has been picked in Karen Read’s second murder trial over the death of her police officer boyfriend, with opening statements scheduled for next week. An 18-member panel — nine men and nine women — was chosen by Tuesday. There will be 12 jurors and six alternates.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Maryland senator to visit El Salvador to check on mistakenly deported man
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D) said he is traveling to El Salvador on Wednesday to check on the status of a deported legal resident.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KRON4

item.title: Bryan Kohberger defense aims to create 'illusion' with renewed bid to toss evidence: fmr. FBI agent
item.description: A former FBI special agent says that Bryan Kohberger's defense team is trying to create an "illusion" for future jurors with a renewed effort to get some evidence tossed.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Suspect in shooting at Dallas high school is in custody, officials say
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — A suspect in a shooting at a Dallas high school that wounded four students and drew a heavy police response to the campus has been taken into custody, school district officials announced Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: The Oklahoma City bombing was 30 years ago. Some survivors worry America didn’t learn the lesson
item.description: Thirty years after a truck bomb detonated outside a federal building in the nation’s heartland, deep scars still remain from the deadliest homegrown attack on U.S. soil.
item.dc:creator: Sean Murphy, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Fort Benning takes back its old name, but to honor a different soldier
item.description: The Army is restoring the name Fort Benning to its storied training post in Georgia, only this time to honor an 18-year-old corporal who fought in World War I rather than a Confederate general. A ceremony to make the name change official was scheduled Wednesday at the base just outside Columbus.
item.dc:creator: CHARLOTTE KRAMON and RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why Utah’s wetlands around the Great Salt Lake may matter more than you think
item.description: Wetlands around the Great Salt Lake are vital to the health of Utah's ecosystem — but did you know that wetlands can also filter out pollutants in the water?
item.dc:creator: Nate Larsen, ABC 4

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: US judge presses Trump administration on its refusal to return Kilmar Abrego Garcia
item.description: GREENBELT, Md. (AP) — A federal judge said Tuesday that she will order sworn testimony by Trump administration officials to determine if they complied with her orders to facilitate the return of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, who was mistakenly deported to a notorious El Salvador prison. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL KUNZELMAN and BEN FINLEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: No jurors picked on first day of Harvey Weinstein’s #MeToo retrial
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As jury selection began Tuesday in Harvey Weinstein’s New York City rape retrial, some prospective jurors made clear they couldn't be fair in judging the one-time Hollywood mogul-turned-#MeToo pariah.
item.dc:creator: JENNIFER PELTZ and MICHAEL R. SISAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NYC Council sues Adams administration over ICE office on Rikers Island
item.description: ICE used to have a presence on Rikers; however, the city’s 2014 sanctuary law effectively banned ICE operations in its jails.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Euzarraga, NewsNation

item.title: A look at the universities with federal funding targeted by the Trump administration
item.description: Harvard University is the latest in a growing list of higher education institutions that had its federal funding targeted by the government in order to comply with the Trump administration's political agenda. The series of threats — and subsequent pauses in funding — to some of the top U.S.
item.dc:creator: MAKIYA SEMINERA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Make-A-Wish Missouri & Kansas grants boy's wish to meet Patrick Mahomes
item.description: Ben got the surprise of a lifetime: the chance to meet his football hero, Patrick Mahomes, thanks to Make-A-Wish Missouri & Kansas.
item.dc:creator: Caroline Pettey, NewsNation

item.title: US measles cases surpass 700 with outbreaks in six states. Here's what to know
item.description: Texas health officials confirmed 20 more cases of measles in its nearly 3-month-old outbreak Tuesday, alongside five new cases in New Mexico and six in central Ohio. Last week, U.S. measles cases topped 700 as Indiana joined five others states with active outbreaks.
item.dc:creator: DEVI SHASTRI, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: A Georgia man was arrested after federal authorities said he made numerous death threats against Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard, the Justice Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, WGN 9

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: “Threatening to harm public officials is a criminal act that cannot be excused as political discourse,” acting U.S. Attorney Richard Moultrie, Jr. said.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, NewsNation

item.title: Tennessee mom tried to hire hit man to kill former TV anchor ex-husband, put him ‘underground’: docs
item.description: Angelia "Angie" Sullivan has been arrested in connection with a murder-for-hire plot against her ex-husband, seeking to have him killed to access trust fund money.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: IRS tax deadline extended in multiple states: What to know
item.description: Residents in all or parts of 13 states can file for an extension to allow disaster-affected areas more time to submit their taxes.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, WGN 9

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: Judge denies bail for man accused of setting fire at Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro's residence
item.description: HARRISBURG, Pa. (AP) — A man who allegedly scaled an iron security fence in the middle of the night, eluded police and set fire to the Pennsylvania governor’s mansion was denied bail Monday as he faced charges including attempted homicide, terrorism and arson.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and MARC LEVY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: White House launches national security investigation into pharma, semiconductors
item.description: The Trump administration disclosed Monday that it had opened an investigation into the effects on national security of importing certain pharmaceuticals, a move widely seen as the prelude to initiating tariffs on prescription drugs.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Weixel, WGN 9

item.title: Boxing legend George Foreman remembered for his love of family, faith and cheeseburgers
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — George Foreman was remembered Monday in a memorial service in his hometown of Houston for his legendary boxing career as well as for his love of God, family, horses and cheeseburgers and for his desire to help his fellow man. “He preached love all the time.
item.dc:creator: JUAN A. LOZANO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: 5.2 magnitude earthquake shakes Southern California
item.description: The quake's epicenter was around two miles south of the community of Julian, about 60 miles east of San Diego.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, WGN 9

item.title: Southern California rocked by a strong earthquake near San Diego
item.description: SAN DIEGO (AP) — Southern California was rocked by a strong earthquake Monday morning near San Diego. The earthquake had a preliminary magnitude of 5.2 and was near the mountain town of Julian, east of San Diego, according to the U.S. Geological Survey.
item.dc:creator: JULIE WATSON, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: NYC helicopter tour company shuts down operations after crash that killed 6
item.description: The helicopter, a Bell 206 L-4, crashed into the Hudson River Thursday, killing all six people aboard.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, ABC 4

item.title: 6 injured after explosion, house collapse
item.description: One house was "leveled" while another "suffered severe collapse damage" following an explosion in Austin on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Julianna Russ, KTLA 5

item.title: Intellectually disabled teen shot by Idaho police dies after being removed from life support
item.description: An autistic, nonverbal teenage boy who was shot repeatedly by Idaho police from the other side of a chain-link fence while he was holding a knife died Saturday after being removed from life support, his family said.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE and REBECCA BOONE, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC
